FIELD DEFINITION: Cotality unique and persistent identification number assigned to each parcel polygon.
Unique identification number assigned to each property.
Two-digit state code representing first two digits of the FIPS county code.
Three-digit county code representing last three digits of the FIPS county code.
Original Assessors Parcel Number (APN) in raw format provided on the assessor tax roll.
An Alternate ID provided by some counties that can be used to identify a property.
Assessor's Parcel Number in an unformatted form. This is most often used by the county and others as a unique key.
Full name of property owner - 1st occurrence.
Original Address.
Original USPS city where parcel is located.
Original USPS state where parcel is located.
USPS zip where parcel is located.
Standardized property address.
The city associated with the property address.
The two-letter USPS postal abbreviation associated with the state / protectorates / commonwealth (e.g., CA, VI).
Code assigned by the USPS. This is populated by various source files and other proprietary and non-proprietary processes. Data may be the 5-digit zip or 9-digit Zip+4 (e.g., 00501 or 954630042).
The digits found to the left of a street name, representing the house number for the mailing address.
The characters found to the immediate right of a house number (e.g., 202B Jones Rd).
The second set of numbers in an address range.
This field represents the direction found to the left of the street name (e.g., 9340 N Dunhill Dr). AKA Directional Abbreviations, Pre-Directional.
The name or number of the street of the mailing address.
The Mode or Type of street found to the right of the street name (e.g., 9340 N Dunhill Dr). AKA Street Designators / Street Suffixes.
The quadrant is found to the right of Mailing Mode (e.g., 2040 NW 100 ST SW). AKA Post-Directional.
The unit or suite number of the mailing address (e.g., 649 Lake Shore Dr #1400).
The city associated with the mailing address.
The two-letter USPS postal abbreviation associated with the state / protectorates / commonwealth (e.g., CA, VI).
Code assigned by the USPS. This is populated by various source files and other proprietary and non-proprietary processes. Data may be the 5-digit zip or 9-digit Zip+4 (e.g., 00501 or 954630042).
This is the four digit code used by the local mail carrier to identify the delivery path.
Full mail address (not including City/St/Zip) (e.g. 123 N Main St).  This also contains unparsed foreign mailing address.
CoreLogic standardized and parsed name for the person receiving mail on behalf of the owner.
A "Y" represents a record where the property owner has contacted the DMA (i.e., Direct Marketing Association) and has asked not to be contacted by mail for solicitation.
ESPG:4326 coordinate for latitude in decimal degrees (in millionths of degrees) of either the centroid of the parcel shape geometry or a point that falls inside.
ESPG:4326 coordinate for longitude in decimal degrees (in millionths of degrees) of the parcel centroid.
Geohash encoding of the latitude and longitude coordinates, which are either the centroid of either the centroid of the parcel shape geometry or a point that falls inside.
ESPG:4326 coordinate for latitude in decimal degrees (in millionths of degrees) of a point within the parcel. May be different than the above parcel centroid.
ESPG:4326 coordinate for longitude in decimal degrees (in millionths of degrees) of a point within the parcel. May be different than the above parcel centroid.
Geohash encoding of the latitude and longitude coordinates, which are either the centroid of the parcel shape geometry or a point that falls inside. May be different than the above parcel centroid.
Date the parcel shape was first identified by Cotality.
Date the parcel record was most recently updated.
Unique data set identifier used for linking records to their relevant metadata entry
Geometry/Coordinates in GeoJSON format
Unique data set identifier used for linking records to their relevant metadata entry
Geometry/Coordinates in WKT (Well Known Text) format

A CoreLogic defined, persistent, 5-digit code that uniquely defines every county or county equivalent.  In some cases, the Corelogic County Code is based on FIPS code but may represent a prior version.
This internal sequence number is used to ensure uniqueness of the Assessor's Parcel Number.

Unique property key, which can be used to link to other files associated with property (used in Legacy Product).   This is a fixed-width field, with the APN component padded with blanks out to 45 characters within the composite key.
The current 5-digit Federal Information Processing Series codes used nationally that uniquely defines every county or county equivalent.
The county or source number used primarily for billing. Note: this number is typically not unique to a parcel, but rather to a tax bill (multiple parcels can be combined on 1 bill).
APN that would link to CoreLogic online products.
Comprised of Census Tract, Census Block Group, Census Block, and Census Block Suffix. These numbers are established by the US Department of Commerce (Bureau Of The Census).
The "range" portion of geographical coordinates based on local surveys. Ranges typically run east or west of pre-determined "meridian" in six mile intervals.
The "township" portion of geographical coordinates based on local surveys. Townships typically run north or south of pre-determined "meridian" in six mile intervals.
The "section" portion of geographical coordinates based on local surveys. Sections are 1 square mile and there are 36 sections within the intersection of a Range / Township.
A section that has been divided into four "sub" sections (e.g., NE, NW, SE, SW).
A CoreLogic established Land Use code converted from various county Land Use codes to aid in search and extract functions.
The data contained in this field is based upon County and/or Local established Zoning Codes and is not converted by CoreLogic.
A CoreLogic general code used to easily recognize specific property types (e.g., Single Family Residence, Condominium, Commercial).
This field is populated with a "Y" if a Manufactured Home is present on the parcel.
Total number of buildings on the parcel.
Positive or negative aspects associated with the location of the parcel (e.g., Waterfront, Flood Plain, Airport).
View from building (e.g., Gulf, Mountains, Pool).
Type of electricity and/or type of wiring within the building (e.g., Average Wiring, Underground Wired, Private Source).
Type of fuel used for heating of water and building (e.g., Solar, Gas, Oil).
Type of sewer system on the parcel (e.g., Public, Septic, Commercial).
Type of water service on the parcel (e.g., Public, Well, Cistern).
Identifies the county block number where the parcel is located. Part of a subdivision or tract legal description. Subdivisions are often broken down into blocks, which are then broken down into lots.
Subdivision or County Map Lot Number. Part of a subdivision or tract legal description. Subdivisions are often broken down into blocks, which are then broken down into lots.
The unique number assigned to a specific subdivision by the county. Often this is used in lieu of an actual subdivision name.
The first component of a recording system used by some counties to catalog subdivision / condo plans (e.g., Book 123 Page 55A).
The second component of a recording system used by some counties to catalog subdivision / condo plans (e.g., Book 123 Page 55A).
The name of the Subdivision or Condominium where the parcel is located (e.g., Highland Mills Estates, Crystal Towers Condo).

County or local jurisdiction legal property descriptions. This description provides additional information regarding the property. It primarily delineates the exact boundaries of the property and may include other values like mineral rights, description of the layout of the property, etc.

County or local jurisdiction legal property descriptions. This description provides additional information regarding the property. It primarily delineates the exact boundaries of the property and may include other values like mineral rights, description of the layout of the property, etc.

County or local jurisdiction legal property descriptions. This description provides additional information regarding the property. It primarily delineates the exact boundaries of the property and may include other values like mineral rights, description of the layout of the property, etc.

County or local jurisdiction legal property descriptions. This description provides additional information regarding the property. It primarily delineates the exact boundaries of the property and may include other values like mineral rights, description of the layout of the property, etc.
The "TOTAL" (i.e., Land + Improvement) Value closest to current market value used for assessment by county or local taxing authorities.
The "LAND" Value closest to current market value used for assessment by county or local taxing authorities.
The "IMPROVEMENT" Value closest to current market value used for assessment by county or local taxing authorities.
The code appearing in this indicator field reflects the type of values used to seed the TOTAL VALUE CALCULATED field (i.e., A = Assessed, M = Market, P = Appraised, T = Transitional).

FIELD NAME: CALCULATED TOTAL LAND VALUE SOURCE CODE
FIELD DEFINITION: The code appearing in this indicator field reflects the type of values used to seed the LAND VALUE CALCULATED field (i.e., A = Assessed, M = Market, P = Appraised, T = Transitional).
The Total Assessed Value of the Parcel's Land & Improvement values as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
The Assessed Land Value as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
The Assessed Improvement Value as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
The Total Market Value of the Parcel's Land & Improvement values as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
The Market Land Value as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
The Market Improvement Value as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
The Total Appraised Value of the Parcel's Land & Improvement as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
The Appraised Land Value as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
The Appraised Improvement Value as provided by the county or local taxing/assessment authority.
Total property tax amount for the current tax year.
Property tax amount less exemptions and/or credits.
The tax year for which the taxes were billed.
The assessment year for which the taxes were billed.
Identifies the tax area code associated with the parcel.
County provided tax rate for the property based on Property Taxes billed through the county.
County provided value of the improvement applied to the parcel used in the calculation of the tax bill.
County provided land value of a parcel used in the calculation of the tax bill.
County provided value of fixture, features or miscellaneous values used in the calculation of the tax bill.
Assessed Value less exemptions or credits.
The linear feet across the front of the lot - facing the street.
The linear feet between the front and back of the lot.
CoreLogic derived or county provided value that indicates the total acreage of the parcel.
Total land mass in square feet.
A textual description of the lot area, which may include size and unique attributes of the lot (e.g., irregular lot size).
The Building Square Footage that reflects the most accurate available for use in assessments or comparables (e.g., Living, Adjusted, Gross).

FIELD NAME: UNIVERSAL BUILDING SQUARE FEET SOURCE CODE
FIELD DEFINITION: The codes appearing in this field indicates the source used to populate the UNIVERSAL BUILDING SQUARE FEET field.A=ADJUSTED, B=TOTAL, D=GROUND FLOOR, G=GROSS, L=LIVING, M=BASE/MAIN
The size of the building in square feet. This field is most commonly populated as an aggregate total when a county does not differentiate between living and non-living areas.

FIELD NAME: TOTAL LIVING AREA SQUARE FEET - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: This is the area of all buildings that are used for general living. This is typically the area of all buildings that is heated or air conditioned and generally does not include Garage, Porch or Unfinished Basement Square Footage values.
Square footage of the part of the building which is level with the ground (typically the front of the building). This is generally above the basement(s) and below the second floor.
This is the square footage for the entire building. Typically this represents all square feet under the roof.

This is the square footage used by the county or local taxing / assessment authority to determine Improvement Value. This figure is typically 100% of the living area, plus lower percentage of non-living area.
This is the total square footage associated with the Basement portion of a building. This would include both finished and unfinished areas.
This is the total square footage of the primary garage or parking area.

This is the construction year of the original building on the parcel as provided by the county.  Not all counties maintain both Original and Effective Year Built. Null if not available from the source.  Format: YYYY

Typically this field reflects the first year the building was assessed with its current components (e.g., a building is originally constructed in 1960 and a bedroom and bath was added to the building in 1974. In this example, Year Built would be 1960 and the Effective Year Built would be 1974).   Certain counties only maintain one Year Built data element, and overlay Original Year Built with Effective Year Built if there was a structural change to the building that affected Assessed Value.  In these counties, Year Built will be null and Effective Year Built will contain the single data element provided by the source - examples include Fresno and San Diego counties.  Format: YYYY

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF BEDROOMS - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: CoreLogic calculated total bedrooms for all the buildings on the property.

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF ROOMS - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: Total number of rooms as supplied by the source.

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF BATHROOMS - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: Total number of rooms that are considered bathrooms across all buildings on the property.  For example, a half bathroom is considered one room.

Total number of rooms that are Bathrooms (Primary Building) if this is the method of establishing bathroom property characterized by the source.  Source values are whole numbers, although field allows for up to 2 decimal places in data deliveries.

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF FULL BATHS - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: Total number of full bath rooms as supplied by source. If source does not break out full versus partial Baths -field will be null in this case.

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF HALF BATHS - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: Total number of half bath rooms (typically comprised of a sink & toilet) as supplied by source. If source does not break out full versus partial Baths – field will be null in this case.

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF 1QTR BATHS - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: Total number of quarter bath rooms (typically comprised only of a sink, as found in many laundry rooms) as supplied by source. If source does not break out full versus partial Baths – field will be null in this case.

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF 3QTR BATHS - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: Total number of three-quarter bath rooms (typically comprised of a sink, toilet & shower stall) as supplied by source.  If source does not break out full versus partial Baths – field will be null in this case.
The total number of bathroom fixtures - Primary Building (typically a full bath would have 4 fixtures - 1 = sink, 1 = toilet, 1 = bathtub, 1 = shower head).

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF BATH FIXTURES - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: CoreLogic calculated total designated bath fixtures associated with all the bathrooms in all the buildings on the property. Examples: sink, toilet, shower stall, tub and faucets.
The standardized code that identifies the basement's degree of completion.
This field is populated with a "Y" if a fireplace is located within the building.
This represents the number of fireplace openings located within all buildings on the property.
The type of fireplace. If more than one fireplace, this field reflects the first/main building.
The type of floor construction (e.g., Concrete, Plywood).
Type / number of stories (e.g., Split Foyer, Tri Level, 2 Story).
Number of stories associated with the building.
The primary building type (e.g., Bowling Alley, Supermarket).
The primary improvement type (e.g., Grain Silo, Hanger, Marina).
This represents the physical condition of the main improvement (e.g., Good, Fair, Under Construction).
The primary method of construction (e.g., Steel / Glass, Concrete Block, Log).
The type and/or finish of the exterior walls (e.g., Vinyl Siding, Brick Veneer, Frame / Stone).
The type of foundation (e.g., Continuous Footing, Pier, Mud Sill).
The type of wall framing used (e.g., Bar Joist, Reinforced Concrete, Flexicore).
Type of garage or carport present (e.g., Attached Finished, Enclosed Carport, Basement Garage).
Type or method of heating (e.g., Hot Water, Heat Pump, Baseboard, Radiant).
This represents the total number of parking spaces or car capacity associated with the garage or parking type.
Type of parking found on the parcel. This is typically commercial or communal in nature (e.g., Condos).
This field is populated with a "Y" if a Pool is present on the parcel.
Type of pool, construction or pool amenities (e.g., Kidney, Gunite, Vinyl, Jacuzzi, Heated).
Type of construction quality of building (e.g., excellent, economical).
Type of roof covering (e.g., Clay Tile, Aluminum, Shake).
Type of roof (e.g., Gambrel, Gable, Flat, Mansard).
Type of building style (e.g., Colonial, Cape Code, Bungalow).

FIELD NAME: TOTAL NUMBER OF UNITS - ALL BUILDINGS
FIELD DEFINITION: Number of residential, apartment or business units.
A binary field indicating the parcel is near a major railway. Detailed metrics like shortest distance, intersection status, number of intersections are available in our Proximity Parcels product.
A binary field indicating the parcel is near a major road. Detailed metrics like shortest distance, intersection status, number of intersections are available in our Proximity Parcels product.
A binary field indicating the parcel is near a transmission line. Detailed metrics like shortest distance, intersection status, number of intersections are available in our Proximity Parcels product.
A binary field indicating the parcel is near a wetland. Detailed metrics like shortest distance, intersection status, area overlap are available in our Proximity Parcels product.
A binary field indicating the parcel is near a protected area. Detailed metrics like shortest distance, intersection status, area overlap are available in our Proximity Parcels product.

The most predominant crop type covering the parcel, as classified by the USDA Crop Land Data Layer. Detailed metrics for top 5 crop types and their percentages are available in our Parcel Terrain product.
FEMA flood zone designation boundary covering largest portion of the parcel. Detailed metrics for top 5 flood zone types and their sub-types are available in our Parcel Terrain product.
2020 census tract, a small statistical subdivision of a county defined by the U.S. Census Bureau.
2020 census block group, a cluster of census blocks within a tract that shares demographic characteristics.
U.S. Census provided unified school district in which the center of the parcel is located
Count of unique addresses associated with the property. Detailed address information is available within the Address Connect product

The Primary Business Use Indicator is a proprietary, rules-based attribute designed to distinguish between addresses that are dedicated business premises (e.g., retail storefronts, offices, manufacturing facilities) and those that are not primarily used for business (e.g., home-based businesses). Possible values are T/F for true and false. True represents the address is a dedicated business premises, and false indicates the address is not primarily used for business.

Composite risk value from 1 to 100, based on the combined average annual loss rates for multiple perils, relative to all residential properties nationwide.  The included perils are: earthquake, wildfire, inland flood, severe convective storms, winter storms, storm surge and hurricane wind.  A value of 1 indicates perils are modeled but there is no risk. A value of NULL indicates insufficient data to determine the perils for this property.
Date of the parcel lineage / change event.

A CoreLogic system generated indicator used to advise customers how to process records contained in delta files.  Values are: A = ADD, Customer will do an UPSERT, U = UPDATE, Customer will do an UPSERT, D = DELETE, Customer will do a DELETE, BLANK = No Action Required.  BLANK is used for Initial Delivery Files or Refresh Files.
